en.extract:
The olive-headed greenbul (Arizelocichla striifacies) is a species of the bulbul family of passerine birds. It is native to the eastern Afromontane.


Taxonomy
The olive-headed greenbul was formally described in 1895 by the German ornithologists Anton Reichenow and Oscar Neumann under the binomial name Xenocichla striifacies. The specific epithet combines the Latin stria meaning "furrow" of "striation" with facies meaning "countenance" or "appearance". This species is now one of 11 greenbuls placed in the genus Arizelocichla that was introduced in 1905 by the American ornithologist Harry C. Oberholser.
Two subspecies are recognised:

A. s. striifacies (Reichenow & Neumann, 1895) – southeast Kenya to southwest Tanzania (Udzungwas)
A. s. olivaceiceps (Shelley, 1896) – southwest Tanzania (Rungwe), Malawi and northwest Mozambique
The two subspecies were formerly sometimes treated as separate species with the English name "stripe-faced greenbul" used for the nominate subspecies and "olive-headed greenbul" for A. s. olivaceiceps.


References

The '''olive-headed greenbul''' ('''''Arizelocichla striifacies''''') is a species of the [[bulbul]] family of [[passerine]] [[bird]]s. It is native to the eastern [[Afromontane]].

==Taxonomy==
The olive-headed greenbul was [[species description|formally described]] in 1895 by the German ornithologists [[Anton Reichenow]] and [[Oscar Neumann]] under the [[binomial name]] ''Xenocichla striifacies''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Reichenow | first1=Anton | author-link=Anton Reichenow | last2=Neumann | first2=Oscar | author2-link=Oscar Neumann | year=1895 | title=Diagnosen neuer, von Oskar Neumann in Ost- und Central -Afrika entdeckter Yogelarten | journal=Ornithologische Monatsberichte | volume=3  | issue=5 | pages=73-76 [74] | language=German | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/8747630 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Greenway | editor2-first=James C. Jr | year=1960 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=9 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology  | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts  | page=258 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14480933 }}</ref> The specific epithet combines the [[Latin]] ''stria'' meaning "furrow" of "striation" with ''facies'' meaning "countenance" or "appearance".<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=striifacies | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=striifacies | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=5 September 2024 }}</ref> This species is now one of 11 greenbuls placed in the genus ''[[Arizelocichla]]'' that was introduced in 1905 by the American ornithologist [[Harry C. Oberholser]].<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=August 2024 | title=Bulbuls | work=IOC World Bird List Version 14.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=5 September 2024}}</ref>

Two [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''A. s. striifacies'' ([[Anton Reichenow|Reichenow]] & [[Oscar Neumann|Neumann]], 1895) – southeast Kenya to southwest Tanzania (Udzungwas)
* ''A. s. olivaceiceps'' ([[George Ernest Shelley|Shelley]], 1896) – southwest Tanzania (Rungwe), Malawi and northwest Mozambique

The two subspecies were formerly sometimes treated as separate species with the English name "stripe-faced greenbul" used for the [[nominate subspecies]] and "olive-headed greenbul" for ''A. s. olivaceiceps''.<ref name=ioc/>
